articles.title: DAX Down Sharply As U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Fail
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - German stocks tumbled on Monday as oil prices climbed higher and bond yields rose after peace talks between the United States and Iran failed to bring about an agreement, and the U.S. Navy moved to block maritime traffic to and from Iran through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Rate hike bets increase after the failed US-Iran talks as energy prices remain elevated
articles.expand: The optimism that briefly buoyed global markets last week has vanished as traders firm up rate hike bets for major central banks following the collapse of the US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad this weekend. The breakdown of negotiations has reignited fears of a protracted conflict, ending the fragile hope that a resolution was within reach. WTI crud...

articles.title: China new bank loans slow further in the first quarter this year
articles.expand: New yuan loans for the month of March came in at ¥2.99 trillion, missing on estimates of ¥3.4 trillion. That brings the total new bank lending in Q1 2026 to roughly ¥8.6 trillion. The total there marks a drop from Q1 2025 of around ¥9.8 trillion. Typically, the first quarter of the year is when we normally see the big credit and liquidity boost in ...

articles.title: European Shares Decline After US Warning Over Hormuz
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - European shares edged lower on Monday after weekend peace talks in Islamabad failed and the U.S. Navy moved to block maritime traffic to and from Iran through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls seem hesitant as oil prices rally on failed US-Iran talks
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts fresh sellers following an intraday move higher at the start of a new week and now seems to have stalled its recovery move from sub-1.3800 levels, or a nearly two-week low set on Friday.

articles.title: US Dollar Index rebounds to 99.00 as oil price recovery prompts hawkish Fed risks
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which gauges the Greenback’s value against six major currencies, trades 0.25% higher to near 99.00 during the European trading session on Monday.

articles.title: ECB: Earlier hikes as core risks rise – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists Anatoli Annenkov, Michel Martinez, Fabien Bossy and Sam Cartwright argue that upside risks to Euro area core inflation justify bringing forward European Central Bank rate hikes.

articles.title: WTI Crude Oil stalls below $98.00 with Iran peace hopes still alive
articles.expand: Oil prices jumped on Monday following the failure of the peace talks between the US and Iran and the US pledge to block the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: BoJ Ueda Cites Dual Impact of Oil Prices, Keeps Markets Guessing on Next Rate Hike
articles.expand: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda struck a cautious tone, warning that rising crude oil prices and instability in global financial markets could pose fresh risks to Japan’s outlook. Speaking via remarks delivered by Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino, Ueda emphasized the need for “vigilance” as Middle East tensions intensify, noting that ener...

articles.title: Oil prices jump back above $100 after US-Iran negotiations breakdown, Trump's blockade
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW Oil prices opened higher today following the breakdown of US-Iran negotiations over the weekend. This shouldn’t be a surprise though given the strong divergence in stances ahead of the talks. There have been reports of US and Iran continuing to exchange messages through diplomatic backchannels, but for now we don’t have any off...

articles.title: Not yet the start of a downtrend for the dollar - Credit Agricole
articles.expand: Credit Agricole was already skeptical about any positive developments from US-Iran talks and their call was vindicated after seeing how things transpired over the weekend. The negotiations broke down and the US is now placing their own blockade on the Strait of Hormuz. It is already seeing oil prices race above $100 to start the new week, with fear...

articles.title: EUR/HUF: Regime change supports forint outlook – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Tatha Ghose highlights that Hungary’s election delivered a supermajority for Peter Magyar’s Tisza party, easing transition risks and opening scope for structural reforms.

articles.expand: Bank of Japan (BoJ) Governor Kazuo Ueda said in his prepared remarks in a speech during the European trading session on Monday that the economic recovery is modest in the wake of the war in the Middle East.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Market pricing de-escalation hopes – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Thu Lan Nguyen notes that negotiations between the United States and Iran have failed while Washington threatens a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, risking a sharper global shortage of Oil and gas.

articles.title: Pound Sterling bounces off low; GBP/USD lacks follow-through beyond 1.3400 on firmer USD
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair opens with a bearish gap at the start of a new week and, for now, seems to have snapped a five-day winning streak to its highest level since late February, around the 1.3485 region set last week.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar pares losses vs USD as rallying oil prices counter hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair retreats a few pips from the Asian session high and currently trades around the 1.3860-1.3855 region, up around 0.15% for the day.

articles.title: Chart Art: NZD/USD Reversal to Gain Traction After Pullback?
articles.expand: After breaking above a long-term descending trend line, NZD/USD went into correction mode to gather more bullish energy. Can buyers still sustain the reversal?

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Middle East ceasefire hangs by a thread
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair came back to life this week, settling a handful of pips above the 1.1700 mark. Risk-on flows began late Tuesday, when United States (US) President Donald Trump withdrew his threat to destroy Iran.

articles.title: ‘Fertiliser for friends’: Jakarta fix quiet assurance to ease farming crisis
articles.expand: Australia has secured quiet assurances from Indonesia that it will be a reliable supplier of fertiliser and urea amid critical global shortages due to the Middle East war, as farmers planting winter crops face a crisis without guaranteed access to crucial supplies.

articles.title: NBP expands gold reserves and moves closer to the global Top 10
articles.expand: The NBP increased its gold reserves in March 2026 by about 13 tons, taking advantage of a sharp price correction. Total holdings rose to around 580 tons. Despite a lower valuation due to falling prices, the central bank continues its strategy of accumulation and is now just over 30 tons away from entering the global top 10 in gold reserves.

articles.title: USD/CAD Forecast: Bears turn cautious ahead of US-Iran talks, US CPI, Canadian jobs data
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair gains some positive traction on Friday, snapping a four-day losing streak to the 1.3800 neighborhood or over a two-week low set the previous day. Doubts over the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire support the safe-haven US Dollar (USD) and act as a tailwind for spot prices.
